name: Farfalle
recipeIngredient: Garlic (as much as you like), crushed or minced
Portobello mushrooms (or any other wild mushrooms)
Greens (arugula, swiss chard, spinach, etc.), chopped leaves only
Farfalle pasta (bowties) - other types work just as well
recipeInstructions:
1. Roast the red peppers until charred on the outside. Let them sit in a paper bag for about 15 minutes to allow the skin to peel off easily. Remove the outer layer of skin while running under cold water, then cut off the top, slice in half, and remove the seeds.
2. In a pan, pour about 1/3 cup broth and bring to a boil.
3. Sauté the garlic until it softens.
4. Add red wine and cook down for about 5 minutes.
5. Add mushrooms, sauté them, then add more broth and wine to keep the pan covered.
6. Once the mushrooms have cooked down sufficiently, add the rest of the broth, chopped greens, and sliced red peppers. Cook until the greens are wilted. The broth can be reduced further for thickness.
7. Add the cooked pasta to the sauce and stir.
This recipe is a flavorful combination of roasted red peppers, garlic, mushrooms, and fresh greens tossed in farfalle pasta with a rich red wine sauce.
